name: APT39
summary: Iranian MOIS-linked cyber espionage cluster associated with Rana Intelligence Computing/Chafer activity, focused on surveillance and collection of personal, travel, telecommunications, academic, and government data using spearphishing, public-facing web compromise, OWA credential abuse, HTTP/DNS C2, proxying, credential theft, local staging, and exfiltration through C2, BITS, FTP, and web-service channels.
extra_hunts: Review OWA, VPN, and public-facing web-server access for stolen-credential use, SQL injection, ANTAK/ASPXSPY-style web shells, and unusual admin sessions followed by internal discovery.

Hunt for Rana/Chafer HTTP C2 patterns such as /update.php?req= with &m=d, &m=u, or &m=b parameters, /asp.asp?ui= host fingerprints, /service.html retrievals, and anomalous HTTP requests containing backslashes or host/user identifiers.
Inspect BITS activity for unusual SecurityCenterUpdate or HelpCenterUpload transfers, /test.asp or asp.asp?ui= traffic, and outbound uploads using actor-controlled HTTP or FTP infrastructure.
Pivot from WinRAR/7-Zip staging, C:\Windows\Help\OEM or C:\Windows\debug\WIA data stores, and .tmp/CAB artifacts into HTTP, BITS, FTP, Dropbox, or C2-channel exfiltration.

Hunt for persistence and collection artifacts including UpdatMachine/UpdateMachineG scheduled tasks, CacheTask_* tasks, AppInit_DLLs/RequireSigned registry changes, %APPDATA%\Microsoft\Event Cache modules, and Microsoft\Feed or Microsoft\Taskbar dn/up folders.

Review lateral movement from compromised web or OWA hosts using RDP, SSH, SMB admin shares, PsExec/RemCom/xCmdSvc, CrackMapExec, Mimikatz, Ncrack, and custom SOCKS/proxy tooling such as REDTRIP, PINKTRIP, or BLUETRIP.
